# dynamodb-toolkit-express — extended reference

> Express adapter for `dynamodb-toolkit` v3. One middleware factory, zero runtime dependencies.

## Scope

A thin translation layer between Express's `(req, res, next)` handler shape and `dynamodb-toolkit`'s framework-agnostic pieces:

- `matchRoute(method, path, methodPrefix)` from `dynamodb-toolkit/handler` — pure route-shape recognizer.
- Parsers from `dynamodb-toolkit/rest-core`: `parseFields`, `parseSort`, `parseFilter`, `parsePatch`, `parseNames`, `parsePaging`, `parseFlag`.
- Builders + policy from `dynamodb-toolkit/rest-core`: `buildEnvelope`, `paginationLinks`, `defaultPolicy`, `mergePolicy`, `mapErrorStatus`, `buildErrorBody`.
- A consumer-supplied `dynamodb-toolkit` `Adapter` instance.

## API

```ts
import type {Request, RequestHandler} from 'express';
import type {Adapter} from 'dynamodb-toolkit';
import type {RestPolicy} from 'dynamodb-toolkit/rest-core';

interface ExpressExampleContext<TItem extends Record<string, unknown> = Record<string, unknown>> {
  query: Record<string, string>;
  body: unknown;
  adapter: Adapter<TItem>;
  framework: 'express';
  req: Request;
}

interface ExpressAdapterOptions<TItem extends Record<string, unknown> = Record<string, unknown>> {
  policy?: Partial<RestPolicy>;
  sortableIndices?: Record<string, string>;
  keyFromPath?: (rawKey: string, adapter: Adapter<TItem>) => Record<string, unknown>;
  exampleFromContext?: (context: ExpressExampleContext<TItem>) => Record<string, unknown>;
  maxBodyBytes?: number;
}

function createExpressAdapter<TItem extends Record<string, unknown> = Record<string, unknown>>(
  adapter: Adapter<TItem>,
  options?: ExpressAdapterOptions<TItem>
): RequestHandler;
```

## Routes

Rooted at the mount point (`app.use('/planets', createExpressAdapter(adapter))` strips the prefix natively):

| Method | Path                | Adapter method                   |
| ------ | ------------------- | -------------------------------- |
| GET    | `/`                 | `getAll` → envelope + links      |
| POST   | `/`                 | `post`                           |
| DELETE | `/`                 | `deleteAllByParams`              |
| GET    | `/-by-names`        | `getByKeys`                      |
| DELETE | `/-by-names`        | `deleteByKeys`                   |
| PUT    | `/-load`            | `putAll`                         |
| PUT    | `/-clone`           | `cloneAllByParams` (body=overlay)|
| PUT    | `/-move`            | `moveAllByParams` (body=overlay) |
| PUT    | `/-clone-by-names`  | `cloneByKeys` (body=overlay)     |
| PUT    | `/-move-by-names`   | `moveByKeys` (body=overlay)      |
| GET    | `/:key`             | `getByKey`                       |
| PUT    | `/:key`             | `put` (URL key merged into body) |
| PATCH  | `/:key`             | `patch` (meta keys → options)    |
| DELETE | `/:key`             | `delete`                         |
| PUT    | `/:key/-clone`      | `clone`                          |
| PUT    | `/:key/-move`       | `move`                           |

Wire contract — envelope keys, query syntax, meta-key prefixes, status codes — is identical to `dynamodb-toolkit/handler`. Override via `options.policy`.

## Dispatch

- Unknown route shape → `next()`. Other middleware can respond; Express's default 404 fires otherwise.
- Known shape, unsupported method → `405 Method Not Allowed`.
- Thrown errors map through `policy.errorBody(err)` + `mapErrorStatus` into a JSON body plus the matching status. Unexpected post-response failures are forwarded to `next(err)` so the Express error pipeline can finish the socket.

## Body parsing

- If `req.body` is set (e.g. via `express.json()`), it is used as-is. The body-parser's own byte cap applies.
- Otherwise the raw Node request stream is consumed with a `maxBodyBytes` cap (default **1 MiB**, measured in bytes via the shared `readJsonBody` from `dynamodb-toolkit/handler`). Over-cap → `413 PayloadTooLarge`; malformed JSON → `400 BadJsonBody`.
- Write routes (POST /, PUT /:key, PATCH /:key) require a JSON object body; `null` / arrays / primitives reject with `400 BadBody`.

## Query coercion

Express's default `qs` parser can produce nested objects and arrays of objects in `req.query`. `rest-core` parsers expect plain strings, so the adapter coerces at the boundary: string values pass through, `string[]` values collapse to the first entry (matching `URLSearchParams.get`), anything else is dropped.

## Compatibility

- **Express 4** and **Express 5** (peer `^4.21.0 || ^5.0.0`). Async errors are surfaced through `next(err)` regardless of Express version.
- **Node 20+**, **Bun**, **Deno** — the test suite runs identically on all three.

## Reference

- Parent toolkit: https://github.com/uhop/dynamodb-toolkit
- Sibling adapter (Koa): https://github.com/uhop/dynamodb-toolkit-koa
- Route pack / wire contract: https://github.com/uhop/dynamodb-toolkit/wiki/HTTP-handler
- AGENTS.md: conventions for contributors and AI agents.
